en.extract:
Bannerman's weaver (Ploceus bannermani) is a species of bird in the weaver family, Ploceidae.  It is native to the Western High Plateau. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.  It is threatened by habitat loss.
Its scientific and common names honor the ornithologist David Armitage Bannerman.

'''Bannerman's weaver''' ('''''Ploceus bannermani''''') is a [[species]] of [[bird]] in the weaver family, [[Ploceidae]].  It is native to the [[Western High Plateau]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is [[subtropical]] or [[tropical]] moist [[montane forest]]s.  It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" />

Its scientific and common names honor the [[ornithologist]] [[David Armitage Bannerman]].<ref>{{cite book|last=Beolens|first=Bo|title=Whose Bird? Men and Women Commemorated in the Common Names of Birds|year=2003|publisher=Christopher Helm|location=London|page=38}}</ref>
